# TODO: textedit R1 violation — cursor-cell count != active cursor count

## Status

Found by `make test-fuzz` (FuzzSessionWithFile, 1m fuzztime) during the A4
gate run in worktree agent-a37ae6463df6a3a3e. **Pre-existing, NOT an A4
regression**: the minimized failing input reproduces byte-identically at
commit b66b8fb (before any A4 changes):

```
session_fuzz_test.go:319: invariant R1: cursor-cell count 3 != active cursor count 2
```

The R1 invariant is defined in internal/fuzz/ui/textedit/textedit.go
(cursor-cell count == active cursor count while the editor is focused; see
also selection_helpers.go's EOL-positioned-cursor accounting). The bug is in
textedit/markdownedit cursor rendering — OUT OF SCOPE for the A4-A7
workspace-guard worker (a parallel agent owns textedit/markdownedit), so it
is recorded here rather than fixed or silently skipped.

The failing corpus entry is deliberately NOT committed to testdata (that
would leave `make test` permanently red for everyone, exactly like the
reverted FuzzSaveRace entry 229c0ba/fa7b014). Recreate it from the base64
below.

## Repro

```sh
mkdir -p pkg/ui/pages/workspace/testdata/fuzz/FuzzSessionWithFile
base64 -d > pkg/ui/pages/workspace/testdata/fuzz/FuzzSessionWithFile/a2426bd8f5490fff <<'B64'

B64
go test -tags fuzzing -count=1 -run 'FuzzSessionWithFile/a2426bd8f5490fff' ./pkg/ui/pages/workspace
```
